title: Complaint filed against woman for protesting traffic jam during BJP rally in Mumbai
content: A complaint has been lodged with Mumbai Police against a woman who confronted Maharashtra minister Girish Mahajan during a BJP rally in Worli over traffic congestion caused by the event. The complaint, filed by Zen Sadavarte—daughter of lawyer Gunratna Sadavarte—alleges that the woman disrupted the rally, used abusive language, and created public disturbance.

title: 'Happy Birthday but ... ': Arjun Tendulkar wishes father Sachin - Watch
content: Arjun Tendulkar shared a funny birthday wish for Sachin Tendulkar, saying, "Happy Birthday, I am not going to eat this because I don't eat sugar very often. I will see you soon." As Sachin turned 53, fans celebrated his legendary career, highlighted by 34,357 international runs, most centuries, and a lasting legacy as one of cricket’s greatest icons.

title: CCTV captures chaos at Iran hospitals amid strikes; patients and staff seen scrambling
content: Surveillance footage reveals chaotic scenes in Tehran hospitals during air strikes, with medical staff urgently evacuating patients, including newborns carried in fruit baskets. The ongoing Iran conflict, sparked by Operation Epic Fury, has escalated tensions in the Persian Gulf, disrupting oil shipments and leading to naval confrontations.

title: KPMG to cut 10% of US audit team after failed retirement efforts
content: KPMG is set to cut approximately 10% of its US audit partners after voluntary retirement schemes failed to yield desired results. The accounting giant, one of the Big Four, cited a need to align its partnership size with current business demands.

title: Stock market crash: Why have Sensex, Nifty dropped over 1% in trade today? Top reasons
content: Technology stocks bore the brunt of the selling. Shares of Infosys, HCLTech, Tech Mahindra and Tata Consultancy Services fell between 2 per cent and 4 per cent after Infosys’ fourth-quarter results failed to meet market expectations.

title: With Polgar, Gukesh's coach as followers, 12-yo Sarbalia reshapes chess as content
content: 12-year-old Aarav Sarbalia is gaining recognition as a rising chess talent. Alongside his on-board progress, his structured social media content has attracted prominent followers, including Judit Polgar and D Gukesh's coach, Vishnu Prasanna. Supported by his family and guided by top coaching, Aarav is building momentum toward his long-term goal of achieving the Grandmaster (GM) title.

title: Gambler, psychopath, Rs 7 lakh debt: What we know about Delhi IRS officer’s daughter's killer
content: A former domestic help, Rahul, allegedly raped and murdered a civil services aspirant in South Delhi. Driven by debt from gambling, addiction, and resentment after losing his job, Meena used his knowledge of the victim's home to gain entry.

title: After $6.25bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750m
content: Michael and Susan Dell have pledged $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This significant donation will focus on integrating AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education. The project, expected to open by 2030, aims to enhance Austin's healthcare capabilities and advance medical innovation.

title: PSL toss turns WWE! Mascots ‘fight’, Ramiz Raja’s commentary steals show – Watch
content: A pre-match mascot scuffle at the Pakistan Super League's Karachi Kings vs. Lahore Qalandars clash provided hilarious entertainment, with commentator Ramiz Raja's witty narration stealing the show. Later, Khushdil Shah's explosive 44 off 14 balls, including 37 in the last 10 deliveries, powered Karachi to a thrilling five-wicket victory chasing 200, keeping their tournament hopes alive.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' falls short of Rs 20 crore at end of week 5; total collections hit Rs 1,766 crore
content: Blockbuster 'Dhurandhar 2' is experiencing a slowdown, hitting its lowest collection of Rs 1.40 crore net on Thursday, its 36th day. After a phenomenal opening and crossing Rs 674 crore in its first week, the film's collections have steadily declined. Despite this, its worldwide gross collection is an impressive Rs 1,766.06 crore.

title: Nike to lay off 1,400, majority cuts in tech team; COO says 'not a new direction'
content: Nike is laying off 1400 employees globally, primarily in its technology division, as part of its 'Win Now' plan to streamline operations and ensure long-term growth. The company is modernizing its technology, manufacturing, and supply chain, consolidating key hubs and improving integration. Affected employees will receive direct communication and support.

title: 'Will win 110 seats in phase-1': Amit Shah predicts BJP sweep after record turnout in Bengal polls
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah stated that the high voter turnout in the first phase of the West Bengal assembly elections signals a desire for change. He asserted that the Bharatiya Janata Party is poised for victory and will form the next government with an absolute majority.

title: Gold price prediction: Will prices recover today & what should investors do?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices continue to be volatile and a buy on dips strategy is recommended for today, says Jateen Trivedi, VP Research Analyst - Commodity and Currency, LKP Securities recommends a buy on dip strategy.

title: Iran admits 'strategic mistake' over nuclear issue at negotiation table in Pak
content: In a bold declaration, an MP from Iran has criticized the recent diplomatic discussions held in Pakistan, deeming them a 'tactical blunder.' He contended that engaging in dialogue about Iran's nuclear ambitions has undermined Tehran's negotiating position. The MP disclosed that the US had imposed a demand for a two-decade suspension on enriched uranium activities, which Iran firmly turned down.

title: No PSL for Mustafizur Rahman as Bangladesh board withdraws NOC
content: Bangladesh have prioritised player fitness after the ODI series against New Zealand, ruling Mustafizur Rahman out of PSL 2026 due to injury and sending him for scans and rehab. Nahid Rana has also been held back to prepare for the Pakistan Tests. With key pacers rested, Bangladesh named a fresh T20I squad for the New Zealand series, handing debut call-ups to Abdul Gaffar Saqlain and including Ripon Mondal to test new talent.

title: Infosys share price today: Stock falls around 4% after Q4 numbers; what’s the outlook?
content: Brokerage views on Infosys remain mixed, with analysts balancing near-term challenges against the company’s long-term positioning. They have expressed a measured outlook on Infosys.

title: ‘He knew we needed him’: Gaikwad dedicates CSK win to Mukesh after mother’s passing
content: Chennai Super Kings captain Ruturaj Gaikwad dedicated their 103-run victory over Mumbai Indians to teammate Mukesh Choudhary, who played despite his mother's recent passing. Gaikwad praised Choudhary's courage, while Sanju Samson's unbeaten 101 powered CSK to 207/6. Spinners Akeal Hosein and Noor Ahmad then restricted MI to 104, securing a dominant win.

title: 22 militants killed in northwest Pak clashes after army firefight near Afghan border
content: Pakistani security forces killed 22 alleged militants in Khyber District during an operation that also resulted in the death of a 10-year-old child. This incident occurs amidst a rise in militant attacks, particularly in provinces bordering Afghanistan, and ongoing tensions between Pakistan and Kabul.

title: 'I left school at 11, washed utensils for Rs 60': Munawar Faruqui recalls his struggling days
content: Bigg Boss 17 winner Munawar Faruqui revealed his challenging childhood, starting work at nine and leaving school at eleven to wash utensils for a meager wage. He shared his father's fear of him becoming a driver, stemming from his own 30-year career.

title: Boat ride, camera in hand: PM Modi shares Hooghly moments; 'Maa Ganga' message from Bengal — watch
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi shared moments from his visit to the Hooghly river banks in Kolkata, including a boat ride. He expressed gratitude to Maa Ganga and met with local boatmen and morning walkers. The visit occurred amidst ongoing political campaigning for the West Bengal assembly elections, with the BJP aiming to unseat the ruling Trinamool Congress.

title: Instagram user's 'jaldi aa jaiye' message gets a prompt reply from Amit Shah in Bengal
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah's social media interaction with a supporter waiting for his roadshow in West Bengal went viral. Shah, who had posted a sunset video with a swipe at the ruling TMC, apologized for the delay to a user waiting in the heat. He later held the roadshow, highlighting public sentiment against TMC's alleged atrocities.

title: Before South Delhi IRS officer’s daughter’s murder, accused raped his friend’s wife
content: A 22-year-old IRS officer's daughter was found dead in Delhi, allegedly by a former domestic helper. Hours before this crime, the accused reportedly committed a sexual assault in his native Alwar village. Investigators are piecing together a rapid sequence of events across two states, fueled by alleged gambling debts.

title: Russini’s husband faces scrutiny over claims he knew of alleged Vrabel affair
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and the former New York Times’ reporter and NFL insider, Dianna Russini, continue to dominate headlines as their alleged affair rumors swirl. As Dianna Russini has stepped down from her role at The Athletic, new allegations have been made about her marital life that she has always tried to keep out of the spotlight.

title: Final Instagram post of Brazilian triathlete Mara Araujo resurfaces after death
content: Brazilian triathlete Mara Flavia Araujo died during the swimming leg of the Ironman Texas triathlon on April 19, 2026. Her last Instagram post, shared hours earlier, has gone viral after her death. Reports from Chosun.com and Yahoo confirm she went missing during the swim. A close friend revealed she was unwell before the race but still chose to compete.

title: 'He fathered her child': Claims about Russini’s son spark new Vrabel saga twist
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, has publicly announced that he is taking some time off to seek counselling as the alleged affair rumors with Dianna Russini refuse to die down as new details emerge. Amid all the mess, it seems the situation has taken a turn for the worse as Dianna Russini’s husband and child have been dragged into the drama.

title: Will MS Dhoni play the next game for CSK? Michael Hussey shares new updates
content: Chennai Super Kings fans are still waiting for MS Dhoni’s return in IPL 2026 as he continues to recover from a calf injury. Despite training regularly, he has missed nearly a month. Batting coach Michael Hussey dismissed rumours of Dhoni playing only as an impact sub, saying he will keep wickets. “I'm pretty sure he'll be behind the stumps,” Hussey said, adding Dhoni is progressing well but will return only when fully fit.

title: Asian stocks today: Markets on edge as US-Iran tensions, oil surge weigh on sentiment
content: Asian markets navigated a cautious trading session Friday, influenced by ongoing Iran conflict uncertainties and stalled US-Iran talks. While some indices saw modest gains, others dipped, reflecting investor unease.  Rising oil prices added to market anxiety, with analysts predicting continued volatility.  Attention also turned to currency markets, particularly the Japanese yen, and upcoming central bank policy decisions.

title: Kraken was real and devoured dinosaurs? School-bus-sized giant octopus ruled ancient seas
content: Scientists have uncovered evidence of enormous, intelligent, octopus-like predators that dominated Earth's ancient oceans. These creatures, some up to 19 meters long, possessed bone-crushing beaks and rivalled top predators like mosasaurs. This discovery challenges previous understandings of prehistoric marine ecosystems, suggesting invertebrates played a more dominant role than previously believed.

title: Daily-wager’s daughter Nausheen Naz has no gear, eyes hockey Asia Cup
content: Nausheen Naz, a 15-year-old hockey prodigy, trains for the Under-18 Asia Cup. Hailing from Seoni, she overcame poverty and used a broken stick to hone her skills. The MP Hockey Academy provided her with training and equipment. Naz is now India's exciting women's hockey forward prospect. Her journey highlights talent emerging from humble beginnings.

title: ‘Felt humiliated after sacking’: Accused had ill intentions towards IRS officer’s daughter
content: A 23-year-old man, Rahul, is accused of a brutal rape and murder of an IRS officer's daughter in Delhi, allegedly committing another sexual assault in Alwar hours prior. Familiar with the victim's home, he used a spare key, attacked her, and stole valuables. He confessed to the crime, citing money as motive.

title: Nashik TCS row deepens: 4 accused held in fresh case; employee’s wife also 'targeted'
content: Nashik police have apprehended four TCS employees, already in judicial custody, on fresh charges of sexual harassment and religious insult. The latest case stems from a male employee's complaint, alleging his wife was also harassed. This brings the total to nine cases against eight TCS staff, with investigations ongoing.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei awaiting prosthetic leg after strike, suffered severe facial burns
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei faces significant medical challenges post-bomb attack, impacting his direct engagement. This has amplified the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' influence, with analysts suggesting a shift towards a 'board-style' leadership where military commanders hold sway.  The situation casts uncertainty over ongoing ceasefire talks and diplomatic pathways.

title: PSL drama! Foreign coach loses cool, starts swearing in presser - Watch
content: Islamabad United coach Luke Ronchi lost his temper during a PSL 2026 press conference after his team’s six-wicket defeat to RawalPindiz. Interrupted mid-answer, he snapped, "He can ask me a question, I don't give a s**t." RawalPindiz secured their first win after eight losses, while Islamabad United remain in playoff contention with two matches left.

title: IPL 2026: Why concussion sub for a shoulder injury? Mahela Jayawardene explains
content: Mumbai Indians faced controversy in their IPL 2026 loss to Chennai Super Kings after using Shardul Thakur as a concussion substitute for Mitchell Santner. Coach Mahela Jayawardene defended the decision, citing a head injury, though Santner later wore a shoulder sling. The debate overshadowed MI's continued struggles on the field.

title: Chennai conundrum: Lower turnout, yet higher ‘percentage’ in Tamil Nadu elections
content: Chennai's high voter turnout percentage masks a decline in absolute votes cast compared to previous elections. This surge is attributed to a significantly reduced electorate following revisions. Despite fluctuating voter numbers, the city has historically seen shifts between major parties, with current contests potentially becoming multi-cornered, making vote distribution crucial for outcomes.

title: 'Layered system of threats': How Iran’s use of 'fast-attack ships' is choking Hormuz
content: Iran's recent use of swarms of fast boats to seize container ships near the Strait of Hormuz is raising new maritime security concerns. These operations, part of a layered threat system, challenge previous assessments of Tehran's naval capabilities and highlight difficulties in securing the vital oil route.

title: 'Kedarnath Kabhi mat aana': Agitated man shares video as wife and child seen crying
content: As Kedarnath Dham reopens, a viral video highlights the overwhelming stress faced by pilgrims due to overcrowding and logistical challenges. While authorities implement safety measures, miscommunication and immense crowds can turn sacred journeys into exhausting ordeals. Careful planning and patience are crucial for a fulfilling pilgrimage experience amidst the spiritual rush.

title: Bengal polls: Empty polling booths, scarred villages at ground zero of SIR rage
content: Mothabari voters, many with EPIC cards, were turned away on polling day, their names deleted from electoral rolls despite prior voting and document submissions.  Migrant workers returned hoping to rectify their status, only to find their names still missing. This exclusion cast a shadow over the democratic process, leaving many feeling disenfranchised and questioning the fairness of the system.

title: Top shares to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Bharat Electronics, and Colgate-Palmolive (India) have been recommended as the top stocks to buy today (April 24, 2026) by Bajaj Broking Research. Take a look at the target prices and expected returns:

title: Oil prices today: Crude climbs as US-Iran talks uncertainty keeps markets on edge
content: Middle East tensions surged Friday, pushing oil prices up as Iran released footage of commandos boarding a ship in the Strait of Hormuz and reported military activity over Tehran. Analysts warn prolonged disruption could severely impact global supply, with inventories potentially falling to multi-year lows.  Markets remain on edge as diplomatic efforts face uncertainty.

title: Kerala Assembly polls: IUML legislator Abdul Hameed slams Congress over CM debate
content: IUML Malappuram district general secretary P. Abdul Hameed, MLA, warns such talks could ‘disappoint’ UDF supporters in Kerala

title: AP Ministers continue Singapore training on governance, development models
content: The six-Minister delegation, on a week-long official visit from April 20 to 27 under the Swarna Andhra Vision 2047, heard former Minister Lim Swee Say outline Singapore’s shift from a ‘nation-first’ to a ‘we-first’ governance model.

title: Reviving Panchayati Raj system more urgent than raising Lok Sabha strength to ensure personal political survival: Congress
content: On Panchayati Raj day, the Congress also said that it is fully committed to strengthening the spirit of Gram Swaraj and congratulated the village heads and members of the panchayats and local bodies across the country

title: How Folksoul brings marginalised women artistes together
content: Founded by rapper Krantinaari and musician Charu Hariharan, the collective channels stories of identity and resilience through its performances.

title: Pahalgam, a year on: Resilience falters, tourism struggles
content: After the initial shock and grief of the April 2025 terrorist attack that killed 26 unarmed men on south Kashmir’s Baisaran meadow, Peerzada Ashiq finds that locals grapple with an uncertain future. Tourism is low, livelihoods have been lost, and heightened security measures are in place

title: APPCB sets up help desk at Ongole for industrialists filing pollution permits
content: The desk will help industrial units in Prakasam and Bapatla districts file applications on the APOCMMS portal and reduce delays in obtaining ‘Consent for Establishment’ and ‘Consent for Operation’ approvals.

title: LDF sees Indira Canteen proposal as bid to undermine Samridhi@Kochi
content: LDF criticises Indira Canteen proposal, claiming it undermines Samridhi@Kochi’s efforts to provide affordable meals in Kochi

title: Samridhi vs Indira Canteen row erupts again in Kerala amid political slugfest over food sourcing
content: Budget initiative by Kochi Corporation and Kudumbashree faces controversy as LDF accuses UDF of misusing food from Samridhi to support Indira Canteen. Kochi Mayor claims the arrangement ‘benefits the women behind Samridhi’. The new dispute is sparked by LDF leader V.A. Sreejith’s social media claims

title: T.N. polls: Accessible elections remain out of reach for persons with disabilities
content: As most polling stations were situated in schools, the grounds were filled with sand or gravel, making it unsuitable for wheelchairs to navigate

title: Supreme Court refers AgustaWestland middleman James' bail plea to another Bench
content: The Delhi High Court had on April 8 dismissed the petition of the British national in the case, saying there was no merit in the plea of Mr. James who was extradited from Dubai in December 2018

title: Riots 2020: High Court dismisses Delhi Police appeal against bail to Ishrat Jahan
content: "Considerable period of more than four years has passed since the impugned order and there is no allegation that the respondent has in any manner violated the terms of the bail. We are not inclined to interfere with the impugned order. The appeal is accordingly dismissed," says the Bench

title: Army chief Upendra Dwivedi inducted into International Hall of Fame at U.S. Army War College
content: General Dwivedi, a distinguished fellow of the U.S. Army War College, is the third Indian Chief of Armed Forces to be inducted into the International Hall of Fame after General V.K. Singh and General Bikram Singh

title: BJP has sought to 'finish' off Nitish: Tejashwi in Bihar Assembly
content: We knew that the BJP would not let Nitish Kumar remain the CM. The BJP kept saying that Nitish would be the NDA's leader till 2030. But now they have finished him off, says RJD working president Tejashwi Yadav

title: Get out, you're causing traffic jam: Woman snaps at Minister during BJP's Mumbai rally
content: The incident occurred on April 21, when Mahajan was addressing reporters at the rally, organised to target Opposition parties over the defeat of the Constitution Amendment Bill, which aimed to fast-track the implementation of 33% reservation for women in legislative bodies, in the Lok Sabha

title: ‘Anga, Banga, Kalinga’ will have BJP governments next month: Amit Shah
content: The Union Home Minister said a Bengal-born and Bengali-speaking person will be the Chief Minister of West Bengal when the BJP comes to power

title: Tamil Nadu elections: Voters upset over SIR-related deletions across State
content: A resident says that her family filled out the forms and submitted all the required documents, but the names were removed from the list after SIR exercise. We did not get any response even after we reached out to several officials, she adds

title: Guntur Municipal Corporation wins UNIDO’s 4th Gender Equality Mobilisation Award in Vienna
content: The award, presented at the International Day of Women in Industry 2026 celebrations, recognises the civic body’s deployment of 200 women as electric auto drivers for door-to-door garbage collection; GMC has also earned State-level recognition for property tax collection.

title: Troubled waters: A.P., T.N. fishermen embroiled in a row
content: The seizure of four mechanised boats owned by Tamil Nadu fishermen at Juvvaladinne Fishing Harbour in Nellore district by local fisherfolk has prompted the Andhra Pradesh government to reinitiate boat patrolling across four coastal districts, bringing to the fore yet again a four-decade-old issue of illegal intrusion. The conflict takes a political turn and the repercussions are witnessed in 164 A.P. villages, narrates N. S. Chowdary

title: Another ‘godman’ in Nashik faces allegations of sexual exploitation, booked
content: Kakde also allegedly clicked her objectionable photographs and threatened her with dire consequences in case she complained against him, the woman told police

title: India approves ₹30 billion for Maldives under SAARC swap
content: In a statement, the Maldives Foreign Ministry said the settlement of the $400 million facility reflects the government’s commitment to meeting its financial obligations

title: Make sure medicines, vaccines are available: CM
content: CM Rekha Gupta said that deficiencies in cleanliness, medicines and basic facilities would not be tolerated under any circumstances.

title: Bihar Government seeks ₹12,000 crore loan from RBI to cover delayed social security pensions
content: Tejashwi Yadav claimed the State government is bankrupt, but Deputy Chief Minister Vijay Kumar Choudhary dismissed this, saying it’s routine for the government to take loans for development

title: Gunmen kill Congress leader’s son, injure another at his home in Chhattisgarh
content: The Congress leader’s son Aayush Kashyap (19), who sustained two gunshot wounds to the chest, died on the spot, while his younger brother Ashutosh (16) suffered a bullet injury to his hand, the police said.

title: Andhra Pradesh Health Department’s AI-driven medtech pilot yields early results; 12,677 patients screened in 37 days
content: Shortlisted through the MedTech Innovation Challenge, the startups have been piloting their technologies across 18 government hospitals; the best performers will be awarded cash prizes and a ₹1 crore work order.

title: Gauhati High Court rejects Pawan Khera’s anticipatory bail plea
content: On April 21, 2026 the Gauhati High Court reserved its order on Congress leader Pawan’s Khera anticipatory bail plea

title: Activist Gurjeet Khalsa brought down from BSNL tower in Patiala after 560 days
content: Mr. Khalsa decided to come down after the Punjab government recently notified the anti-sacrilege law – the Jaagat Jot Sri Guru Granth Sahib Satkar (Amendment) Act, 2026 – which proposes stricter punishment, including life imprisonment and a fine of up to ₹25 lakh, for any act of sacrilege against the Guru Granth Sahib.

title: Karnataka’s new excise regime calibrates taxes to social cost of alcohol
content: Social cost that has been calculated to be around ₹1,270 per litre of alcohol in beverage in 2024-2025 and is estimated to around ₹2,000 by 2028-2029

title: Domestic work and its quest for recognition
content: Despite Karnataka notifying the Code on Wages Rules under the broader Code on Wages, 2019 framework, aimed at standardising minimum wages, working hours and employer responsibilities, and a draft Domestic Workers (Social Security and Welfare) Bill, domestic workers remain outside the formal workforce, writes Rishita Khanna.

title: Why Mangaluru MLA asked Priyank Kharge about Kalaburagi’s last rank in SSLC examinations in Karnataka
content: Mangaluru City North MLA Y. Bharat Shetty suggests Mr. Kharge to improve education system in Kalaburgi instead of criticising Dakshina Kannada

title: One killed, two admitted as group of people falls into pit at under-construction site in Mumbai’s Bhandup
content: The incident took place at Metro Mall near Janata market. The pit belonged to a building being constructed by the Slum Rehabilitation Authority

title: Economic growth, prosperity remain primary impulse for changes in India: Vinay Kwatra
content: The Ambassador to the U.S. said India has experienced consumption products, technology, services and knowledge being restricted for the country’s growth

title: Double-decker cuts time for ORR commuters, hits Aurobindo Marg traffic
content: The high volume of vehicles is mainly because the flyover directly connects to the Outer Ring Road and South Bengaluru

title: Bengaluru metro services to start early for TCS World 10K on April 26
content: Trains from Bommasandra will commence operations at 3 a.m., followed by services from Madavara at 3.20 a.m., Whitefield (Kadugodi) and Silk Institute at 3.30 a.m., and Challaghatta at 3.35 a.m.

title: Convention to mark 50 years of public life of BSY to be held on May 9, prep to commence on April 26
content: Ground-breaking ceremony for the convention would be held on April 26 to mark the commencement of works related to erecting pandals and setting up temporary infrastructure facilities

title: West Bengal election 2026: Record turnout of over 92% in first phase amidst isolated incidents of violence
content: Before the 2026 elections, the highest turnout was recorded in 2011 when 84.33% voters exercised their franchise; the elections in 2011 saw Trinamool Congress defeating the 34-year Left Front government

title: What Telangana’s survey shows about caste inequality
content: When caste is measured, the gap is not incremental but exponential, with SC and ST households structurally locked in despite economic growth

title: Karur stampede is yet to fade from memory of voters at Velusamypuram
content: Minutes past 2 p.m. Thursday, booth number 15 at the Panchayat Union Middle School, located a few yards away from the scene of the stampede, registered 81.31% polling, indicating that the tragedy has in no way dispirited the enthusiasm of the voters

title: Watch: Manipur sees little respite despite a change of guard under the BJP | The Hindu Editorial
content: A more pro-active role by the Home Ministry and a genuine effort by the BJP to involve all political actors in easing tensions should help. This should be supplemented by a strategy that empowers security agencies to crack down on hardline elements engaging in wanton violence such as bombings.

title: Post-SIR electorate drop drives turnout surge; votes remain steady compared to 2021 election
content: As of 8 p.m. on polling day, voter turnout stood at 83.41%, significantly higher than 59.75% recorded in 2021 Assembly polls, as per ECI data; this is due to reduction in electorate from 40.57 lakh in 2021 to 28.93 lakh in 2026; number of electors who turned up in both elections remains close to 24 lakh

title: Delhi HC questions police over allegations of illegal detention, torture of activists
content: "You may be investigating a terrorist angle, but if you pick up someone, you have to follow the procedure," court said to Delhi Police

title: The real equity gap in higher education
content: Equity in higher education institutions is more constrained by gaps in representation in employment than in admissions; the UGC regulations focus largely on anti-discrimination and complaint-resolution mechanisms, but do not adequately address these structural gaps

title: Kerala on the brink of heatwave, Chief Minister calls for ‘self-lockdown’
content: IMD Director says though heatwave has not been technically declared, the hot and humid conditions are at times comparable to heatwave. Intense heat, coupled with high humidity, is likely to persist at least till April 27

title: Two delivery executives arrested for harassing woman at Yelahanka in Bengaluru
content: The woman was walking near Kogilu Main Road in Maruthinagar when two unidentified youths approached her on a two-wheeler, and allegedly misbehaved with her

title: High healthcare cost, lower ante-natal and post-natal costs in Telangana
content: The high expense of hospitalisation cost is linked to treatment at private hospitals (in urban and rural) which averages ₹72,561

title: Indian-origin US Rep reacts to Trump's anti-India rant
content: the post accused Indian-Americans in the tech industry of not hiring white, native Americans . it also alleges that the Indian immigrants lacked English proficiency . the post received criticism from many Indian-americans across the globe .

title: Mamata Banerjee Says Will Win Delhi After Bengal, Amit Shah Laughs It Off
content: Mamata Banerjee said she will "dismantle" the BJP in the Centre by bring together the Opposition parties.

title: Opinion: Opinion | How Did Munir Get So Close To Trump? Follow Pakistan's Million-Dollar 'Fixers'
content: In just six months in the first half of 2025, Javelin Advisors, just one among many lobbying firms on Pakistan's roll, was paid $200,000, apart from arranging hotels and parties for unknown people. Another was paid $1.5 million to work with the Embas

title: Driver, Who Set Himself Ablaze During Telangana Transport Body Strike, Dies
content: The body of the driver was taken to his relative's village Muttojipet in Warangal district for conducting funeral.

title: Ex-Top Cop R Sreelekha Makes Kerala Assembly Election Debut On BJP Ticket
content: Born and brought up in Thiruvananthapuram, R Sreelekha is the state’s first woman IPS officer and first woman DGP in Kerala.

title: Patriots To Tomahawks: US Blew Through Weapons Worth Billions In Iran War
content: The US military has said it struck more than 13,000 targets, but officials told NYT that the figure masks the vast number of bombs and missiles it used because warplanes, attack planes and artillery typically strike large targets multiple times.

title: "Agar Didi Paise De Deti...": Teen On Why He Killed IRS Officer's Daughter
content: Rahul Meena, accused of killing former employer's 22-year-old daughter, repeatedly said "it just happened". He had come to take money, he said.

title: Punjab Activist Gurjeet Khalsa Ends 560-Day Tower Protest After New Sacrilege Law Enacted
content: Activist Gurjeet Singh Khalsa ends 560-day tower protest after Punjab passes stricter anti-sacrilege law.

title: Filing Income Tax Return? These Mistakes May Attract 200% Penalty

title: "Go To Gym. Do You Have Boyfriend?" Survivor Recalls Harassment At TCS Office
content: The TCS employee has alleged sexual harassment and mental torture by Shahrukh Qureshi and Raza Memon, two of the seven accused arrested in the case.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Behind West Bengal's Record Voter Turnout, A Curious Pattern
content: We have seen enough elections in recent years to know that rising turnout can hurt an incumbent, but it can just as easily consolidate one.

title: "I Doubt He'd Come": Trump As US Plans G20 Invite For Putin
content: The United States is this year's host of the Group of 20 major economies and Trump has promised a grandiose summit in December.

title: Massive Ice Chunk Blocks Mount Everest Route As Climbing Season Begins
content: The obstruction, a towering 100-foot-high chunk of ice known as a serac, is preventing teams from moving beyond a key section of the route near Camp 1.

title: USS George HW Bush Joins Ford, Lincoln: All About 3 US Carriers In Middle East
content: USS George HW Bush was moving along the eastern coast of Africa and was in the Indian Ocean on Thursday, joining the other two carriers in the Middle East.

title: Nagpur Man Collapses After Gym Session, Mother's Alertness Saves Him From Paralysis
content: Diagnostic tests found a blood clot in the 23-year-old's brain, which was removed using a mechanical thrombectomy procedure.

title: Sent To Gurukul, 11-Year-Old UP Boy Returns Home Dead With 40 Injury Marks
content: When Divyansh's body reached his home, family members raised concerns after noticing several injury marks. They alleged that there were around 40 to 45 deep marks on his body, believed to be caused by beating with a stick.

title: Illegal Steroids, Expired Supplements Racket Busted In Delhi
content: Joint task force seized banned steroids and expired supplements from an illegal hub in Delhi's Najafgarh area.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Trump Says US "In No Rush" To End Iran War
content: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: The standoff between the US and Iran has effectively choked off nearly all exports through the strait -- where 20 per cent of the world's traded oil passes in peacetime -- with no end in sight.

title: Indian Navy lists Hormuz as ‘primary area’ of interest, one of the key ‘choke points’
content: Maritime choke points in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR) constitute critical nodes that exert disproportionate influence over global trade and energy security . such choke points are Cape of Good Hope, Mozambique Channel, Bab-el-Mandeb, Suez Canal, Strait of Hormuz, Malacca and Singapore Straits, Sunda Strait, Lombok Strait and Ombai Strait .
